   <item>
      <title>Doc Rivers Said Health Issues, Lack Of Depth, Giannis Antetokounmpo Rumors Undid Bucks Season</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284741/Doc-Rivers-Said-Health-Issues-Lack-Of-Depth-Giannis-Antetokounmpo-Rumors-Undid-Bucks-Season</link>
      <description><p>The Milwaukee Bucks were officially eliminated from postseason contention after their home loss to the San Antonio Spurs on Saturday. Following the games, Bucks coach Doc Rivers was asked about being eliminated and the reasons for the down season for Milwaukee.</p>
<p>"It's been disappointing obviously," Rivers said. "Since I've been here, I haven't had a healthy stretch, and it's been your key guys. It's been Giannis (Antetokounmpo). It's been Dame (Lillard). And you hope you can play through that, but we just haven't had the ability. This year, having only one quote-unquote star, every other team has two and three. We needed health. We were thin. We knew that before the season started and it just didn't go our way. All the talk and all that stuff probably didn't help either."</p>
<p>The last portion of that quote is a reference to the Giannis Antetokounmpo trade rumors and reporting that he was unhappy with the Bucks.</p>
<p>Rivers did say that Milwaukee had some silver linings this season.</p>
<p>"But I always try to look at silver linings and Ryan (Rollins) is one of them. Pete Nance is another one. Ous (Dieng) is one of them. And we gotta rehabilitate or get A.J. Green going again. He's a good player. He's played too many minutes. We've had no choice and I think that's put him in a tough spot and I feel bad for him at times," Rivers said. "Bobby (Portis) is Bobby. He's been a pro throughout this year. We had a great talk today about it before the game. I'm so proud of him as a leader. He tries to do the right stuff. He tries to say the right things in the locker room."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sat, 28 Mar 2026 20:16:26 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284741/Doc-Rivers-Said-Health-Issues-Lack-Of-Depth-Giannis-Antetokounmpo-Rumors-Undid-Bucks-Season</guid>

   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Luka Doncic Faces One-Game Suspension After 16th Technical Foul In Lakers' Win Over Nets</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284727/Luka-Doncic-Faces-One-Game-Suspension-After-16th-Technical-Foul-In-Lakers-Win-Over-Nets</link>
      <description><p>Luka Doncic reached the NBA's suspension threshold Friday, drawing his 16th technical foul of the season during the Los Angeles Lakers' 116-99 victory over the Brooklyn Nets. Barring a successful appeal, Doncic will miss Monday's home game against the Washington Wizards.</p>
<p>The technical was assessed with 5:12 left in the third quarter following a double-foul exchange with Nets forward Ziaire Williams. Video review confirmed Doncic pushed Williams, who retaliated with a swipe at Doncic's face. Crew chief Sean Wright issued technical fouls to both players.</p>
<p>"[Williams] was yelling in my face - three times," Doncic said after finishing with 41 points. "I just wanted to get out of there. It's a double tech, of course. What can I say? I didn't even talk. I just wanted to get out of there. And they said I pushed, my push was 'exaggerated,' which [it] was obviously not. And I don't know what else to tell you."</p>
<p>The incident complicates what has been <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/mvp-candidates">a strong MVP case</a> for Doncic. Over his past 12 games, he has averaged 39.7 points on 48.9% shooting, 8.1 rebounds, 6.9 assists and 2.5 steals, with the Lakers going 11-1 in that span. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284637/LaMelo-Ball-Luka-Doncic-Named-NBAs-Players-Of-Week-22">Doncic was named Western Conference Player of the Week for the second consecutive week Monday</a>.</p>
<p>An appeal is unlikely to succeed. Officials had already conducted a video review before issuing the technical, making a rescission improbable. A prior 16th technical from last weekend, stemming from a confrontation with Orlando's Goga Bitadze, was rescinded after a Lakers appeal.</p>
<p>JJ Redick said he lacked a clear view of the exchange but confirmed, "I'm sure we will appeal it."</p>
<p>The suspension would cost Doncic approximately $317,000, roughly 1/145th of his annual salary. The Lakers defeated Washington by 31 points on Jan. 30, limiting the competitive impact of his absence. Doncic would then return with three days of rest before Tuesday's back-to-back matchup against the Cleveland Cavaliers.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sat, 28 Mar 2026 08:06:56 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284727/Luka-Doncic-Faces-One-Game-Suspension-After-16th-Technical-Foul-In-Lakers-Win-Over-Nets</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Doncic_Luka_lal_251210.jpg" />

      <title>Caleb Wilson Leaves North Carolina To Declare For 2026 NBA Draft</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284726/Caleb-Wilson-Leaves-North-Carolina-To-Declare-For-2026-NBA-Draft</link>
      <description><p>Caleb Wilson, a projected high lottery selection who missed the final nine games of North Carolina's season with hand and thumb injuries, has declared for the 2026 NBA Draft. The 6-foot-10, 215-pound forward announced his decision via Instagram, captioning a photo carousel with "never take it for granted."</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/player/Caleb-Wilson/Summary/200430">Wilson averaged 19.8 points, 9.4 rebounds and 2.7 assists while shooting 57.8% from the field this season</a>. He is widely regarded as part of one of the strongest freshman classes in college basketball history. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/draft/prospects/stats/Advanced_Stats/All/">Wilson ranked second amongst NBA draft prospects in PER behind Cameron Boozer with a mark of 31.2</a>.</p>
<p>The Athletic's draft analyst Sam Vecenie projects Wilson as the fourth overall pick, slotted to the Indiana Pacers in his latest mock draft model.</p>
<p>Wilson's season ended prematurely after he broke his thumb during a practice dunk on March 5, requiring surgery the following day. He had already missed six games earlier with a fractured left hand sustained Feb. 10 against Miami.</p>
<p>Wilson's departure, albeit widely expected, compounds an already difficult stretch for the Tar Heels program. UNC was eliminated in the first round by sixth-seeded VCU and subsequently fired coach Hubert Davis on Tuesday. </p>
<p>Wilson wore No. 8 for the Tar Heels this season, a detail he referenced with the hashtag #8out in his announcement post. </p></description>
      <pubDate>Sat, 28 Mar 2026 08:02:32 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284726/Caleb-Wilson-Leaves-North-Carolina-To-Declare-For-2026-NBA-Draft</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Wilson_Caleb_unc_251223.jpg" />

      <title>Doc Rivers Unsure If Kevin Porter Jr Will Play Again This Season</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284739/Doc-Rivers-Unsure-If-Kevin-Porter-Jr-Will-Play-Again-This-Season</link>
      <description><p>Milwaukee Bucks guard Kevin Porter Jr's season may be coming to an early end. Porter is dealing with synovitis in his right knee. Bucks head coach Doc Rivers said he'd be "surprised" if Porter plays again this season.</p>
<p>Milwaukee went into play on Saturday in 11th place in the Eastern Conference. The Bucks are nine games behind the Miami Heat for the fine Play-In Tournament spot in the East. That means it's just a matter of time before Milwaukee will be officially eliminated from postseason contention.</p>
<p>Porter has averaged 17.4 points, 5.2 rebounds, 7.4 assists and 2.2 steals per game for the Bucks this season. The veteran guard has shot 46.5% from the field this season.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sat, 28 Mar 2026 16:34:13 EDT</pubDate>

   <item>
      <title>Stephen Curry 'Running Out Of Games' To Return This Season</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284730/Stephen-Curry-Running-Out-Of-Games-To-Return-This-Season</link>
      <description><p>Steve Kerr said Friday the Golden State Warriors are running out of games to facilitate Stephen Curry's return from a knee injury, marking the first time the organization has publicly acknowledged the window may be closing on the star guard's season.</p>
<p>"We're not bringing him back for the Play-In game. He needs to play some games, and we need to give him a runway if this is going to work," Kerr said. "And we are running out of games, that's fair to say."</p>
<p>The Warriors listed Curry as day-to-day with what the team has termed "runner's knee." A pregame injury update Friday expressed hope that Curry would participate in 5-on-5 scrimmages in the "coming days." That same target was set last weekend before the training staff opted to allow more recovery time.</p>
<p>Golden State entered Friday at 35-38, occupying the 10th seed in the Western Conference, two games behind the Los Angeles Clippers for eighth. Nine games remain in the regular season.</p>
<p>Curry last played Jan. 30 against the Detroit Pistons. Despite the circumstances, he has remained vocal about his desire to return.</p>
<p>"That's not who we are," Curry said March 15 after a road game against the New York Knicks. "If we have stuff to play for, we play. So I'm working to get back."</p>
<p>Kerr confirmed Curry continues to rehab with the intention of playing. If Curry cannot participate in a full scrimmage next week, however, a season-ending decision appears likely.</p>
<p>Kerr attributed Curry's determination to an innate competitive drive that casual observers often overlook.</p>
<p>"It's just in his blood," Kerr said. "He'll probably be the same way when he's 78 returning from an injury that's affected his golf game. He is an absolute fierce competitor, and you don't teach that. It's just how he's built."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sat, 28 Mar 2026 10:31:41 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284730/Stephen-Curry-Running-Out-Of-Games-To-Return-This-Season</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Curry_Stephen_gsw_250429.jpg" />
   </item>

      <title>Duke Beats St. John's To Advance To Elite Eight As Caleb Foster Delivers Clutch Injury Return</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284722/Duke-Beats-St-Johns-To-Advance-To-Elite-Eight-As-Caleb-Foster-Delivers-Clutch-Injury-Return</link>
      <description><p>Twenty days after fracturing his right foot and 19 days after surgery, Duke guard Caleb Foster came off the bench to score 11 second-half points, lifting the No. 1 overall seed Blue Devils past St. John's 80-75 Friday at Capital One Arena.</p>
<p>The victory sends Duke to its third consecutive Elite Eight where they will face UConn on Sunday. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/basketball/ncaab/march-madness/odds-lines/">The Blue Devils are 5.5 point betting favorites going into the game</a>.</p>
<p>Foster, who had missed five straight games, arrived at the arena on a scooter and departed the postgame press conference the same way.</p>
<p>Duke head coach Jon Scheyer had hoped to use Foster for just 8-10 minutes to rest Cayden Boozer. The Blue Devils ultimately required far more.</p>
<p>St. John's led by 10 points with 15:01 remaining after forcing three turnovers across four consecutive possessions. Scheyer responded with two key adjustments as he deployed a matchup zone defense and inserted Foster, who had gone scoreless through seven first-half minutes.</p>
<p>Foster then scored seven straight points during a decisive 9-2 run and added two critical baskets in the final 2:15. Isaiah Evans led Duke with 25 points, while Cameron Boozer recorded 22 points and 10 rebounds.</p>
<p>The Red Storm connected on 13 three-pointers, their third straight game reaching double digits from distance, with reserve Ruben Prey hitting three first-half threes after making just 14 in 69 career games.</p>
<p>"He had no business playing tonight," Scheyer said. "Ninety-nine percent of guys do not come back to play under the circumstances of what's happened to him. It was incredible the way he willed us. There's no analytics. There's no stats that can measure how big this dude's heart is for what he did."</p>
<p>"As soon as the doctor told me that it's a chance, I just took it and ran with it," Foster said. "I wanted to come out and provide anything possible, experience, whatever the team needed."</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/ncaa/boxscore/2026-03-27/St-Johns-at-Duke/521885">Duke scored 1.238 points per possession in the game</a>.</p>

      <pubDate>Sat, 28 Mar 2026 07:18:19 EDT</pubDate>

   <item>
      <title>Tarris Reed Jr. Delivers At Free Throw Line As UConn Survives Michigan State In Sweet 16</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284723/Tarris-Reed-Jr-Delivers-At-Free-Throw-Line-As-UConn-Survives-Michigan-State-In-Sweet-16</link>
      <description><p>Tarris Reed Jr. overcame a known weakness Friday night, converting all four of his late free throws to help second-seeded UConn defeat third-seeded Michigan State 67-63 in the Sweet 16 at Capital One Arena in Washington.</p>
<p>Reed, shooting just 59% from the line this season, sank two decisive foul shots with 44.8 seconds remaining to give UConn a 63-60 edge. Alex Karaban, who finished with 17 points, then converted both ends of a one-and-one with 22.5 seconds left to stretch the lead to three.</p>
<p>Reed finished with 20 points. His final two free throws, after rebounding a missed Michigan State attempt with 4.6 seconds on the clock, sealed the result.</p>
<p>The Huskies (32-5) advance to Sunday's East Region final against top-seeded Duke, a matchup that mirrors the 1999 national championship game, which UConn won. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/basketball/ncaab/march-madness/odds-lines/">The Huskies are 5.5 point betting underdogs going into the game against Duke</a>.</p>
<p>UConn built an early 19-point advantage on the strength of a 15-1 run, with Malachi Smith, Solo Ball and Jaylin Stewart combining for four three-pointers. Michigan State trimmed the deficit to eight by halftime and pulled ahead 45-44 on a Jaxon Kohler three-point play midway through the second half.</p>
<p>The Spartans (27-8) shot just 4-of-16 from three-point range and ultimately ran out of steam down the stretch.</p>
<p>"Give our guys credit. We bounced back and picked away," Michigan State coach Tom Izzo said. "Our goal in the second half was to play much better. We just kind of wore down."</p>
<p>"I feel like I put in the work. The guys around me were like, 'T, trust. We see you do free throw game every day, you put in the work every day, and we see you at the free throw line,'" Reed said. "So, I took a deep breath and took my time at the line."</p>

      <pubDate>Sat, 28 Mar 2026 07:21:04 EDT</pubDate>

<p>The Eastern Conference playoff race reaches a boiling point this Saturday as the <strong>Philadelphia 76ers</strong> visit the <strong>Charlotte Hornets</strong> at the Spectrum Center. With only a handful of games remaining in the 2025-26 regular season, this matchup carries heavy seeding implications. Philadelphia (40-33) is looking to solidify a top-six spot to avoid the Play-In gauntlet, while Charlotte (39-34) enters as one of the league's hottest teams, currently riding a five-game winning streak. With the season series tied at 1-1, the winner of tonight's contest will secure a vital tiebreaker advantage. Catch the action live on Fubo.</p>

<p>This matchup features a fascinating clash of momentum and star power. The 76ers are finally nearing full health, with <strong>Joel Embiid</strong> and <strong>Paul George</strong> recently returning to the lineup to provide a massive offensive boost. However, the Hornets have transformed into a defensive juggernaut since the turn of the year, posting a 28-11 record in 2026. Led by <strong>Brandon Miller</strong> and the playmaking of <strong>LaMelo Ball</strong>, Charlotte's transition offense and elite three-point shooting (ranking 3rd in the NBA) will be a significant test for Philly's interior defense. You can witness this high-stakes Eastern Conference battle streaming live on Fubo.</p>
<p><a href="https://www.fubo.tv/stream/nba/?irad=1846663&irmp=3589123&subId1=RealGM" target="_blank" rel="noopener"> <input style="background-color: #ea7910; color: #ffffff; border-color: #141414; border-radius: 65px; cursor: pointer; border-width: 3px; padding: 10px; margin: 10px;" type="button" value="LIVE STREAM NBA ACTION ON FUBO" /> </a></p>
<p>Charlotte's surge is anchored by their balanced attack, with <strong>Kon Knueppel</strong> and <strong>Brandon Miller</strong> providing lethal scoring on the wings. All eyes will be on <strong>LaMelo Ball</strong>, who has elevated his game recently, averaging over 24 points per contest during the Hornets' current win streak. For Philadelphia, the priority is reintegrating <strong>Joel Embiid</strong> into the flow; the former MVP looked sharp in his last outing, dropping 35 points in limited minutes.       <title>Sterling Brown Wins First EuroLeague MVP Of The Round Award After 31-Point Game</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284728/Sterling-Brown-Wins-First-EuroLeague-MVP-Of-The-Round-Award-After-31-Point-Game</link>
      <description><p>Sterling Brown earned his first <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/international/league/1/Euroleague/awards/by_season">EuroLeague MVP of the Round</a> distinction in Round 34, delivering 31 points and a PIR of 32 as Partizan Mozzart Bet Belgrade defeated Valencia Basket 110-104 in double overtime at Belgrade Arena.</p>
<p>Brown shot 5-of-10 on two-pointers, 4-of-9 from three-point range and a perfect 9-of-9 from the free throw line. He added 7 rebounds, 2 assists, 1 steal and 6 fouls drawn. His performance made him the first Partizan player to claim the award this season.</p>
<p>Brown played in 268 career NBA games with the Milwaukee Bucks, Houston Rockets, Dallas Mavericks and Los Angeles Lakers across six seasons. Brown last played in the NBA during the 2022-23 season with the Lakers. </p>
<p>Jordan Loyd of Anadolu Efes Istanbul posted the round's highest individual index rating despite coming up short, recording a PIR of 35 in an 82-71 road loss at Real Madrid. He finished with 21 points, 5 rebounds and 6 assists.</p>
<p>Elie Okobo came one point behind Loyd with a PIR of 34 in AS Monaco's 107-97 defeat at Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens. The guard contributed 24 points on efficient shooting and a standout 13 assists.</p>
<p>Zach LeDay of EA7 Emporio Armani Milan and Elijah Bryant of Hapoel IBI Tel Aviv each recorded a PIR of 31 to complete the round's elite individual performances.</p>
<p>Brown's award highlights a competitive Round 34 in which three of the five top-rated individual efforts came in losing efforts, underscoring the tight margins throughout the EuroLeague field.</p></description>

      <title>Yaxel Lendeborg Posts 23/12/7 Line As Michigan Beats Alabama 90-77 To Reach Elite Eight</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284725/Yaxel-Lendeborg-Posts-23-12-7-Line-As-Michigan-Beats-Alabama-90-77-To-Reach-Elite-Eight</link>
      <description><p>Yaxel Lendeborg turned in one of the tournament's most complete individual performances Friday, powering top-seeded Michigan past fourth-seeded Alabama 90-77 in the Sweet 16. The 6-foot-9 forward recorded 23 points, 12 rebounds and seven assists at the United Center in Chicago.</p>
<p>Michigan trailed by nine early and by two at halftime before outscoring Alabama by 15 points in the first 11 minutes of the second half. The Wolverines shot 13-of-27 from three-point range and 50% from the field overall.</p>
<p>Lendeborg hit four three-pointers, orchestrated Michigan's offense in transition and consistently delivered in the interior. A 65-foot outlet pass to Nimari Burnett for a second-half dunk highlighted his varied skill set.</p>
<p>"He's just kind of a Swiss Army knife," Michigan assistant Mike Boynton said. "He can do everything."</p>
<p>Alabama connected on 14 three-pointers but could not sustain its first-half momentum. Burnett, a Crimson Tide transfer, recalled an opposing player shouting that Michigan's shooting pace was unsustainable.</p>
<p>"And we kept it up," Burnett said.</p>
<p>Michigan's victory gave the Big Ten four Elite Eight teams, joining Purdue, Illinois and Iowa, the first time in conference history that has occurred. The Wolverines will face sixth-seeded Tennessee on Sunday with a trip to Indianapolis on the line. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/basketball/ncaab/march-madness/odds-lines/">Michigan are 7.5 point betting favorites over Tennessee</a>.</p>
<p>Michigan last reached the Final Four in 2018 when they lost to Villanova in the national championship. Coach Dusty May acknowledged the broader conference stakes with a smile.</p>
<p>"I would say the best way to break this drought [is] if we just send four Big Ten teams to Indianapolis," May said.</p>
<p>"We're one step closer to our goal," Lendeborg said. "We got a chance to cut down another net [Sunday] and then another one [in Indianapolis]."</p>

      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284724/Tennessee-Reaches-Third-Straight-Elite-Eight-Will-Face-Michigan-After-Routing-Iowa-State-76-62</link>
      <description><p>The sixth-seeded Tennessee Volunteers dismantled Iowa State 76-62 at the United Center in Chicago, setting up a Midwest Region final Sunday against top-seeded Michigan.</p>
<p>Eleven of Tennessee's players are newcomers this season. Coach Rick Barnes assembled the group with clear ambitions from the start.</p>
<p>"I remember the first day, in our meeting with Coach Barnes, he said we could win the national championship," said forward Jaylen Carey, a transfer from Vanderbilt. "We have the team to do it."</p>
<p>Tennessee dominated the interior, holding commanding margins in total rebounds (43-22), offensive boards (16-8) and points in the paint (42-30). Carey and Felix Okpara each recorded double-doubles. Four other Vols grabbed at least four rebounds apiece.</p>
<p>Iowa State played without All-America forward Joshua Jefferson, who was unable to suit up despite working to recover from a sprained left ankle through tipoff. Jefferson told reporters he had only recently begun jogging and still felt pain.</p>
<p>"Just a really tough circumstance to deal with," Cyclones coach T.J. Otzelberger said. "We did everything in our power to get him out there, ready to play, and it just wasn't enough. He's a fierce competitor."</p>
<p>Tennessee's front-court struggles were well documented after last year's Elite Eight loss to Houston, which controlled the glass 42-35 and held the Vols to 29% shooting. Barnes addressed those deficiencies directly in the offseason.</p>
<p>A trip to the program's first Final Four awaits if Tennessee can handle Michigan's formidable front line Sunday. Barnes last reached the Final Four in 2003 with Texas.</p>
<p>"Now four straight Sweet 16s and three straight Elite Eights," athletic director Danny White said. "It's phenomenal."</p>

      <title>Working Bond Of Mitch Johnson, Brian Wright Contributing To Spurs' Rise</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284715/Working-Bond-Of-Mitch-Johnson-Brian-Wright-Contributing-To-Spurs-Rise</link>
      <description><p>The San Antonio Spurs' rise to second place in the Western Conference with a 55-18 record reflects not only roster construction and the emergence of Victor Wembanyama as one of the NBA's best players, but a deeply personal working relationship between head coach Mitch Johnson and general manager Brian Wright, who have been colleagues for nearly a decade.</p>
<p>"I'm fortunate we have a personal history and a personal relationship that allows us to have honest conversations and trust each other and hopefully agree, disagree, and then align," Johnson said of his relationship with Wright.</p>
<p>Both men joined San Antonio in 2016 with Johnson as an assistant with the Austin Spurs and Wright as assistant GM under R.C. Buford. Wright became GM in 2019. Johnson succeeded Gregg Popovich as head coach in 2025 following six years as an assistant and nearly a full season as acting head coach after Popovich suffered a stroke.</p>
<p>That shared climb through the organization's ranks shaped their partnership, Johnson said.</p>
<p>"He's someone I'm very fortunate to have as my boss and someone to work with and partner with," Johnson said of Wright, whom he described as "leader of our project construction and a lot of things that are at the foundational level of our organization from top to bottom."</p>
<p>The bond has produced results. San Antonio ended six consecutive losing seasons without a playoff appearance and has emerged as a legitimate title contender behind Victor Wembanyama. Wright is now considered a leading candidate for NBA Executive of the Year, an honor the franchise last received from Buford in 2016.</p>
<p>"He's a great man and a great leader," Johnson said.</p></description>

   <item>
      <title>NBA Weighing Draft Pick Forfeiture, Million-Dollar Fines As Penalties For Teams Found Tanking</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284714/NBA-Weighing-Draft-Pick-Forfeiture-Million-Dollar-Fines-As-Penalties-For-Teams-Found-Tanking</link>
      <description><p>The NBA is considering severe penalties for teams found deliberately manipulating lineups, player availability and in-game rotations to lose, two league sources told The Athletic. The measures would give Adam Silver broad authority to punish confirmed tanking.</p>
<p>Under the proposed rules, Silver's office could strip a team of its draft pick entirely, relocate it to the end of the lottery or first round, and impose fines reaching into the millions of dollars.</p>
<p>"Without stricter penalties, you could still have crazy behavior," one league source said. "You have to have something in place that is so drastic, a team would actually think twice about tanking. And if a team tries it and gets caught, then the other teams need to see the penalties and realize it isn't worth it to try."</p>
<p>Silver addressed the issue directly at Wednesday's Board of Governors news conference.</p>
<p>"It has business implications. It has basketball implications. It has integrity implications for the league. So it's one that we take very seriously, and we are going to fix it. Full stop. And I want to say that directly to our fans," Silver said.</p>
<p>The penalty proposals accompany <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284708/NBA-Presented-Three-Draft-Lottery-Reform-Options-To-Board-Of-Governors">previously reported lottery reform measures that would expand the draft lottery from 14 to either 18 or 22 teams</a>. Those changes were introduced at this week's Board of Governors meeting and are expected to be voted on in May.</p>
<p>"Exactly what that change is, we're continuing to work on," Silver said. "I think there's also unanimous agreement that we need to make this change in advance of the draft and free agency this year."</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/283924/NBA-Fines-Jazz-$500K-For-Conduct-Detrimental-To-The-League;-Pacers-Receive-$100K-Fine">The NBA fined the Utah Jazz $500,000 earlier this season for "conduct detrimental to the league" after they sat Lauri Markkanen and Jaren Jackson Jr. in the fourth quarter of a competitive game</a>. The Indiana Pacers were fined $100,000 for violating the Player Participation Policy. </p></description>

   <item>
      <title>NBA Executives 'Tilting' Toward AJ Dybantsa Over Darryn Peterson For Top Pick In 2026 Draft</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284713/NBA-Executives-Tilting-Toward-AJ-Dybantsa-Over-Darryn-Peterson-For-Top-Pick-In-2026-Draft</link>
      <description><p>BYU forward AJ Dybantsa has overtaken Kansas guard Darryn Peterson as the frontrunner for the No. 1 pick in the 2026 NBA Draft, according to ESPN's Jeremy Woo.</p>
<p>"Dybantsa has closed the gap and can be considered a highly likely top pick candidate at this point in time based on conversations I've had with NBA executives over the past few weeks," writes Woo. "Based on conversations with sources around the NBA in recent weeks, the probability has been tilting toward Dybantsa as the most likely choice as the No. 1 pick."</p>
<p>Dybantsa's college career ended with BYU's first-round tournament loss to Texas, but he went out posting 37 points, 10 rebounds and a perfect 12-of-12 from the free throw line. Woo credits Dybantsa's improved decision-making, consistent aggressiveness and reliable playmaking for his rising draft stock throughout the season.</p>
<p>Peterson's case, meanwhile, has been complicated by injury, illness and cramping issues that limited his availability and consistency. He averaged 20.6 points over his final nine games but shot just 38.8% from the field and 31.9% from 3-point range. Kansas's season ended Sunday with a buzzer-beater loss to St. John's in the round of 32.</p>
<p>Peterson's elite shotmaking ability keeps him in contention, with teams projecting him in an Anthony Edwards-style role. Duke's Cameron Boozer remains a distant third consideration, though Woo notes he cannot be fully dismissed.</p>
<p>The race between Dybantsa and Peterson is expected to intensify during pre-draft workouts and medical evaluations.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 27 Mar 2026 11:30:59 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284713/NBA-Executives-Tilting-Toward-AJ-Dybantsa-Over-Darryn-Peterson-For-Top-Pick-In-2026-Draft</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Dybantsa_AJ_byu_251212.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>NBA Expansion Plans Driven By 'Ocean Of Capital' Prepared To Invest</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284712/NBA-Expansion-Plans-Driven-By-Ocean-Of-Capital-Prepared-To-Invest</link>
      <description><p>Billionaires and sovereign wealth funds are competing to invest in the NBA as the league moves closer to adding franchises in Seattle and Las Vegas, according to ESPN's Brian Windhorst. Commissioner Adam Silver has not guaranteed expansion, but owner sentiment has shifted decisively in its favor.</p>
<p>"The league believes there's an ocean of capital ready to flow into the league, that in fact billionaires and -- in the case of Europe -- sovereign wealth funds, will be competing to buy in," writes ESPN's Brian Windhorst.</p>
<p>Owners who firmly opposed expansion two to three years ago have reversed course, driven largely by robust financial interest from prospective investors domestically and abroad. The league is also exploring NBA Europe, which would be a separate league operating in Europe and potentially could replace the existing Euroleague.</p>
<p>Recent transactions illustrate the scale of outside money targeting NBA assets. In June 2025, the Buss family sold a controlling stake in the Los Angeles Lakers to Mark Walter, CEO of Guggenheim Partners, at a record $10 billion valuation. Shortly before closing, <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/283494/NBA-Europe-Could-Influence-Future-Policy-On-Sovereign-Wealth-Fund-Stakes">Walter's holding company TWG Global received a $10 billion investment from Mubadala Capital</a>, the asset management arm of Abu Dhabi's sovereign wealth fund. Current NBA rules cap direct sovereign wealth fund ownership at 20% per team, but the Mubadala investment flowed through Walter's private firm, remaining within those limits.</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/283162/QIA-Arctos-Partners-Buying-Stake-In-Wizards-Parent-Company-At-$72-Billion-Valuation">Qatar's sovereign wealth fund QIA separately acquired stakes in the Washington Wizards' parent company in both 2023 and 2025</a>.</p>
<p>Concerns over talent dilution and increased tanking exist among some observers. Those reservations appear secondary, however, to the substantial financial windfalls the league's 30 existing owners stand to collect through expansion fees.</p>
<p>Between two new domestic teams and NBA Europe, <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284684/NBA-Expansion-Fees-From-Las-Vegas-Seattle-Europe-Could-Deliver-$20-Billion-To-Current-Owners">the NBA could see an influx of $20 billion in cash</a> that would be distributed to the 30 owners at a rate of $666 million apiece.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 27 Mar 2026 11:24:29 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284712/NBA-Expansion-Plans-Driven-By-Ocean-Of-Capital-Prepared-To-Invest</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Silver_Adam_nba_220225.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Victor Wembanyama's Spurs Increasingly Viewed As Legit Title Contenders By NBA Scouts, Execs</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284710/Victor-Wembanyamas-Spurs-Increasingly-Viewed-As-Legit-Title-Contenders-By-NBA-Scouts-Execs</link>
      <description><p>The San Antonio Spurs are just two games behind the Oklahoma City Thunder in the Western Conference standings, posting a stunning 23-2 record since Feb. 1. The run has prompted serious debate among NBA scouts and executives about whether Victor Wembanyama and the Spurs can win a title in one of the league's most improbable championship bids in recent memory.</p>
<p>A veteran Eastern Conference scout told ESPN the Spurs have become his preferred contender, citing Wembanyama's growing psychological impact on opponents.</p>
<p>"They're my favorite now," the scout said. "His game is going to be even more dominant in the playoffs and every game he's going to get more and more into your head. In Game 1, your players will think about him on some of their shots. By Game 4, they'll be having full-on nightmares."</p>
<p>Multiple executives echoed that assessment. One whose team faced San Antonio recently was direct in his evaluation.</p>
<p>"They're a very good team. They're very well-coached. They're very well put together," the executive said. "And Victor is absolutely unguardable."</p>
<p>The youth and inexperience concern draws frequent skepticism. But one Eastern Conference executive pushed back firmly.</p>
<p>"Everyone says they don't have experience, but they have a lot more than anyone gives them credit for," one East executive said of the Spurs. "Harrison Barnes and Luke Kornet have championship rings. De'Aaron Fox is one of the league's best clutch players and he's been in the playoffs.</p>
<p>"Do you think Victor is going to be intimidated by the moment? Good luck with that."</p>
<p>One veteran scout acknowledged the tension between historical precedent and Wembanyama's singular ability. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/news/nba-mvp-betting-odds/">Wembanyama has risen to second in NBA MVP odds behind Shai Gilgeous-Alexander</a>.</p>
<p>"History says it doesn't happen this year, and projects against it. But they're different," said the scout. "He's generational."</p>
<p>Potential weaknesses remain as San Antonio's three primary guards De'Aaron Fox, Stephon Castle and Dylan Harper all rank below average as 3-point shooters, a vulnerability playoff opponents will aggressively target. Some of the ways the Spurs have been effective in a regular season will be game planned against in a seven-game series. </p>
<p>The Spurs are currently tied for second with the Boston Celtics for <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/news/nba-championship-betting-odds/">the best NBA championship odds</a> behind the Thunder. San Antonio has a 4-1 record against Oklahoma City this season.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 27 Mar 2026 11:02:57 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284710/Victor-Wembanyamas-Spurs-Increasingly-Viewed-As-Legit-Title-Contenders-By-NBA-Scouts-Execs</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Wembanyama_Victor_sas_241010.jpg" />
   </item>

      <title>Iowa Reaches First Elite Eight Since 1987 After Nebraska's Four-Man Blunder</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284709/Iowa-Reaches-First-Elite-Eight-Since-1987-After-Nebraskas-Four-Man-Blunder</link>
      <description><p>Ninth-seeded Iowa defeated Nebraska 77-71 Thursday in the Sweet 16, reaching the Elite Eight for the first time since 1987. A critical personnel error by the Cornhuskers in the final minute proved decisive.</p>
<p>With 58.8 seconds remaining and Nebraska trailing by three, Alvaro Folgueiras sprinted downcourt completely unguarded while only four Cornhuskers were on the floor. Iowa's Kael Combs delivered a long inbound pass to Folgueiras, who converted a layup and completed a three-point play, pushing the lead to six.</p>
<p>Nebraska coach Fred Hoiberg took full responsibility afterward.</p>
<p>"Put that one on me," Hoiberg said. "It was a miscommunication and I'm the head coach, so put that one on me."</p>
<p>Folgueiras, whose game-winning 3-pointer eliminated top-seeded Florida in the previous round, has now produced clutch performances in back-to-back tournament games. Bennett Stirtz scored 20 points and played all 40 minutes for Iowa, giving him three complete-game efforts this tournament, tied for the most by a Big Ten player over the past 45 seasons per ESPN Research.</p>
<p>Iowa took its first lead on Stirtz's 3-pointer with 2:10 remaining. Tate Sage quickly extended that margin before Nebraska's Pryce Sandfort, a former Hawkeye, answered with a three of his own, setting up the chaotic closing sequence.</p>
<p>The Hawkeyes, the lowest-seeded Big Ten team ever to reach the Elite Eight, previously knocked out Clemson and Florida. Iowa coach Ben McCollum embraced the Cinderella label.</p>
<p>"Yeah, Cinderella, whatever they want to call us. We're in the Elite Eight. That's what they need to call us," McCollum said.</p>
<p>Iowa faces Illinois on Saturday in a battle of Big Ten schools with a trip to the Final Four at stake. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/basketball/ncaab/march-madness/odds-lines/">The Hawkeyes are 7.5-point betting underdogs in the game</a>.</p>

      <pubDate>Fri, 27 Mar 2026 10:55:25 EDT</pubDate>

   <item>
      <title>NBA Presented Three Draft Lottery Reform Options To Board Of Governors</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284708/NBA-Presented-Three-Draft-Lottery-Reform-Options-To-Board-Of-Governors</link>
      <description><p>The NBA presented three NBA Draft Lottery reforms at the Board of Governors meeting this week. Each of the three reforms is designed to combat tanking efforts by teams trying to gain an advantage in the current lottery system.</p>
<p>Option One would see 18 teams in the Lottery. This would feature teams who finish between 7-15 in each conference. The bottom 10 teams would each have an 8% chance of winning the lottery. The remaining eight teams would each have a 2.5% chance of winning the lottery. All 18 picks would be decided by a drawing.</p>
<p>Option Two would see 22 teams in the lottery. This would feature the teams who finish between 7-15 in each conference, plus the teams who are eliminated in the first round of the NBA Playoffs. This version would use a team's two-year combined record to slot them into the lottery order. There would also be a minimum-wins floor (25 was used as an example) in each season. If a team failed to meet that minimum-wins floor, they would be slotted as if they had won the minimum required amount of games.</p>
<p>Option Three would again feature 18 teams in the lottery. This would be a "5x5" lottery. The bottom five teams would have equal odds and a lottery would be held for picks 1-5. That would create a floor for those teams of the 10th pick. The remaining teams would then have a lottery to determine picks 6-18, using teams that aren't in the top-five.</p>
<p>All options are open to modification. The NBA Board of Governors is expected to vote on a new system at their May meeting. This new system will go in effect starting with the 2026-27 NBA season.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 27 Mar 2026 09:13:00 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284708/NBA-Presented-Three-Draft-Lottery-Reform-Options-To-Board-Of-Governors</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/nba_realgm2.jpg" />
   </item>

      <title>Illinois Freshman Duo Posts Historic Double-Doubles In 65-55 Win Over Houston In Sweet 16</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284707/Illinois-Freshman-Duo-Posts-Historic-Double-Doubles-In-65-55-Win-Over-Houston-In-Sweet-16</link>
      <description><p>Third-seeded Illinois held Houston to its lowest scoring output of the season Thursday, beating the second-seeded Cougars 65-55 in the South Region Sweet 16. The Illini (27-8) advance to Saturday's Elite Eight against ninth-seeded Iowa, with a Final Four berth on the line, and are <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/betting/basketball/ncaab/">5.5 point betting favorites in the game</a>.</p>
<p>Illinois broke the game open with a 17-0 second-half run, pushing the lead to 44-26 with roughly 12 minutes remaining. Houston never recovered, at one point going nearly seven minutes without a field goal.</p>
<p>"We were getting stops and we were limiting them to one shot, and to tough shots as well," freshman Keaton Wagler said. "Offensively we were getting the shots that we wanted, we were knocking them down."</p>
<p>David Mirkovic posted 14 points and 10 rebounds, while Wagler contributed 13 points and a game-high 12 boards. The two became the first pair of freshman teammates each to record a double-double in the same NCAA tournament game since freshmen gained full eligibility in 1972-73, per ESPN Research. Andrej Stojakovic, whose father, three-time NBA All-Star Peja Stojakovic, watched from the stands, also added 13 points.</p>
<p>With the performance, <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284132/Illinois-Freshman-Keaton-Wagler-Rises-From-Unranked-Recruit-To-NBA-Lottery-Prospect">Wagler continues his emergence this season from an unranked recruit to a strong NBA lottery prospect</a>.</p>
<p>Houston shot just 34% from the field, far below its usual standard. Projected NBA lottery pick Kingston Flemings managed 11 points on 4-of-10 shooting, while Milos Uzan converted only 2 of 11 attempts.</p>
<p>"We just didn't play good enough," Cougars coach Kelvin Sampson said.</p>
<p>Illinois, playing in its 11th Elite Eight and second in three seasons under coach Brad Underwood, smothered Houston despite finishing well below its season scoring average of 84.7 points.</p>

      <title>Trey Kaufman-Renn's Buzzer Tip-In Sends Purdue Past Texas 79-77 In Sweet 16 Thriller</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284706/Trey-Kaufman-Renns-Buzzer-Tip-In-Sends-Purdue-Past-Texas-79-77-In-Sweet-16-Thriller</link>
      <description><p>Trey Kaufman-Renn tipped in a missed shot by Braden Smith with 0.7 seconds remaining Thursday, lifting No. 2-seed Purdue past 11th-seeded Texas 79-77 in the Sweet 16 at SAP Center. The Boilermakers (30-8) advance to Saturday's Elite Eight against top-seeded Arizona.</p>
<p>Smith had driven toward the basket in the final seconds before missing. Kaufman-Renn, positioned directly beneath the rim, converted the second-chance opportunity.</p>
<p>"I was standing under the rim, I was like 'Please get in there, please get in there,' and it did," Smith said. "Trey tipped it perfectly and I was like 'Thank God, thank God that went in.'"</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/ncaa/daily-leaders/2026-03-26">Kaufman-Renn finished with 20 points</a>, connecting on his first seven attempts and pulling down five rebounds in the first half alone. The senior credited coach Matt Painter's longstanding emphasis on tip-in opportunities for the decisive play.</p>
<p>Texas had tied the game at 77 when Dailyn Swain completed a three-point play with 11.9 seconds left. The Longhorns then pulled 7-foot center Matas Vokietaitis from the floor with 11 seconds remaining after Purdue's Oscar Cluff fouled out, inadvertently creating extra space near the basket.</p>
<p>Injured guard Tramon Mark battled through visible discomfort to post 29 points, which was the highest total by a Texas player in NCAA tournament action since Kevin Durant scored 30 in 2007. The loss was the program's fourth on a game-winning basket in the final two seconds, the most in NCAA tournament history per ESPN Research.</p>
<p>Texas coach Sean Miller made his ninth Sweet 16 appearance without reaching a Final Four. Purdue now faces Arizona with a berth in the program's second consecutive Final Four on the line. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/betting/basketball/ncaab/">Purdue are 5.5 point betting underdogs against the Wildcats</a>.</p>
